dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped 'price breaks above 10-period highest high' to dc_upper (Donchian upper band with 10-period lookback). Mapped '50% above 30-day average volume' to vol_ratio_20 >= 1.5 (volume ratio indicator). Mapped '60th percentile of recent volume' to hvol_20 (historical volatility/volume). Exit condition uses dc_lower (5-period equivalent) for price closing below recent low. Volume expansion sustainability is approximated by the volume conditions. Signal direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT but entry conditions specify long positions, so direction set to LONG.

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped 'price breaks above 10-period highest high' to dc_upper (Donchian upper band with 10-period lookback). Mapped '50% above 30-day average volume' to vol_ratio_20 >= 1.5 (volume ratio indicator). Mapped '60th percentile of recent volume' to hvol_20 (historical volatility/volume). Exit condition uses dc_lower (5-period equivalent) for price closing below recent low. Volume expansion sustainability is approximated by the volume conditions. Signal direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT but entry conditions specify long positions, so direction set to LONG.

source_rule_text: A trader should initiate long positions when price enters new highs accompanied by volume expansion of at least 50% above recent average daily volume, as this signals genuine demand and readiness to move higher.
